verses.text: Paul, 'a prisoner of Jesus Christ,' to Philemon, our dearly beloved fellowlabourer; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes as a prisoner, in love.
Paul, 'a prisoner of Jesus Christ,' to Philemon, our dearly beloved fellowlabourer; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes as a prisoner, in love.
Paul, 'a prisoner of Jesus Christ,' to Philemon, our dearly beloved fellowlabourer; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes as a prisoner, in love.
I thank God, hearing of thy love and faith; thou hast 'refreshed' the saints' hearts. Praised love. He commends Philemon's kindness.
I thank God, hearing of thy love and faith; thou hast 'refreshed' the saints' hearts. Praised love. He commends Philemon's kindness.
I thank God, hearing of thy love and faith; thou hast 'refreshed' the saints' hearts. Praised love. He commends Philemon's kindness.
I thank God, hearing of thy love and faith; thou hast 'refreshed' the saints' hearts. Praised love. He commends Philemon's kindness.
Though I might command, 'for love's sake I rather beseech thee, being Paul the aged.' Gentle plea. He appeals by love, not authority.
Though I might command, 'for love's sake I rather beseech thee, being Paul the aged.' Gentle plea. He appeals by love, not authority.
I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, begotten in my bonds, 'once unprofitable, but now profitable.' Changed slave. Grace made the runaway useful.
I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, begotten in my bonds, 'once unprofitable, but now profitable.' Changed slave. Grace made the runaway useful.
I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, begotten in my bonds, 'once unprofitable, but now profitable.' Changed slave. Grace made the runaway useful.
I would have kept him, but would do nothing 'without thy mind,' that thy kindness be willing. Free consent. He leaves the choice to Philemon.
I would have kept him, but would do nothing 'without thy mind,' that thy kindness be willing. Free consent. He leaves the choice to Philemon.
Perhaps he departed that thou shouldest receive him for ever, 'not now as a servant, but... a brother beloved.' New status. The slave returns a brother.
Perhaps he departed that thou shouldest receive him for ever, 'not now as a servant, but... a brother beloved.' New status. The slave returns a brother.
Receive him as myself; 'if he hath wronged thee... put that on mine account; I... will repay it.' Standing surety. Paul takes the debt on himself, as Christ does ours.
Receive him as myself; 'if he hath wronged thee... put that on mine account; I... will repay it.' Standing surety. Paul takes the debt on himself, as Christ does ours.
Receive him as myself; 'if he hath wronged thee... put that on mine account; I... will repay it.' Standing surety. Paul takes the debt on himself, as Christ does ours.
Let me have joy of thee; 'having confidence in thy obedience.' Confident. He trusts Philemon will do even more than asked.
Let me have joy of thee; 'having confidence in thy obedience.' Confident. He trusts Philemon will do even more than asked.
Let me have joy of thee; 'having confidence in thy obedience.' Confident. He trusts Philemon will do even more than asked.
Epaphras and others salute thee; 'the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.' Farewell. He ends with greetings and grace.
Epaphras and others salute thee; 'the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.' Farewell. He ends with greetings and grace.
